NCT02682927.name: Study 1 (Dravet)
NCT02682927.group: Dravet syndrome, fenfluramine 0.7 mg/kg/d vs placebo (wk 14, >=50% convulsive-seizure responder RR)

allOutcomes.title: Proportion with >=50% reduction in monthly convulsive seizure frequency (0.7 mg/kg arm) (RR)

NCT02682927.snippet: Source: Lagae L et al. Lancet 2019;394:2243-2254 (Study 1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 119 children (age 2-18) with Dravet syndrome and drug-resistant seizures randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to fenfluramine 0.7 mg/kg/d (max 26 mg; n=40), fenfluramine 0.2 mg/kg/d (n=39), or matched placebo (n=40) over 14 weeks as adjunctive to existing regimen. Stiripentol co-use prohibited (tested separately in Study 2).

evidence.label: Primary - Median % Convulsive Seizure Reduction
evidence.text: Median % change in monthly convulsive seizure frequency: 0.7 mg/kg -63.9% vs placebo 1.1% (difference -62.3 pp, 95% CI -75.7 to -47.4; P<0.0001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Responder Rate, Safety
evidence.text: >=50% responder: 0.7 mg/kg 68% (27/40) vs placebo 12% (5/40). No cases of valvular heart disease or pulmonary hypertension on regular echo surveillance through 14 weeks. Decreased appetite 38%, fatigue 16%, diarrhoea 16%.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 92% completion. D4 LOW - central seizure diary review. D5 LOW - SAP locked; mandated echocardiogram monitoring.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 2 Years; max age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or non-pregnant, non-lactating female, age 2 to 18 years, inclusive as of the day of the Screening Visit.~* Clinical diagnosis of Dravet syndrome, where convulsive seizures are not completely controlled by current antiepileptic drugs.~* Must have a minimum # of convulsive seizures per 4-week period for past 12 weeks prior to screening.~* All medications or interventions for epilepsy must be stable for at least 4 weeks prior to screening and expected to remain stab...

NCT02926898.name: Study 2 (Dravet+stiripentol)
NCT02926898.group: Dravet syndrome on stiripentol background, fenfluramine 0.4 mg/kg/d vs placebo (wk 14, >=50% convulsive-seizure responder RR)

allOutcomes.title: Proportion with >=50% reduction in monthly convulsive seizure frequency (0.4 mg/kg arm) (RR)

NCT02926898.snippet: Source: Nabbout R et al. JAMA Neurol 2020;77:300-308 (Study 2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 87 children with Dravet syndrome on stable stiripentol (+ clobazam or valproate) with drug-resistant convulsive seizures randomized 1:1 double-blind to fenfluramine 0.4 mg/kg/d (n=43) or matched placebo (n=44) over 15 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - Median % Convulsive Seizure Reduction
evidence.text: Median % change in monthly convulsive seizures: fenfluramine -54.0% vs placebo -4.3%; treatment difference -49.7 pp (95% CI -66.5 to -26.3; P<0.001). Primary met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Responder Rate, Safety
evidence.text: >=50% responder: 54% (23/43) fenfluramine vs 5% (2/44) placebo. Decreased appetite 44%, fatigue 26%. No cardiac valvulopathy or pulmonary hypertension signals.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 97% completion. D4 LOW. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 2 Years; max age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Subject must be male or non-pregnant, non-lactating female, aged 2 to 18 years (inclusive).~* Subject must have documented medical history to support a clinical diagnosis of Dravet syndrome, where convulsive seizures are not completely controlled by current antiepileptic drugs.~* Subject must be receiving a therapeutically relevant and stable dose of stiripentol (STP) plus clobazam (CLB) and/or valproate (VPA), and for at least 4 weeks prior to screening and be expecte...

evidence.label: Demographics (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Race: Cohort 2 0, Cohort 2 0, Cohort 2 0, Cohort 2 2, Cohort 2 1. NOTE: figures are total-arms aggregate from CT.gov baseline-measurements module; per-arm breakdown available in AACT raw data. RACE caveat: review trial-population racial composition for external-validity considerations.

NCT03355209.name: LGS Study 1
NCT03355209.group: Lennox-Gastaut syndrome, fenfluramine 0.7 mg/kg/d vs placebo (wk 14, >=50% drop-seizure responder RR)

allOutcomes.title: Proportion with >=50% reduction in monthly drop-seizure frequency (0.7 mg/kg arm) (RR)

NCT03355209.snippet: Source: Knupp KG et al. JAMA Neurol 2022;79:554-564 (LGS Study 1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 263 patients with Lennox-Gastaut syndrome randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to fenfluramine 0.7 mg/kg/d (n=87), fenfluramine 0.2 mg/kg/d (n=89), or matched placebo (n=87) over 14 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - Median % Drop Seizure Reduction
evidence.text: Median % change in drop-seizure frequency: 0.7 mg/kg -26.5% vs placebo -7.6%; estimated treatment difference -19.9 pp (95% CI -31.0 to -8.7; P<0.001). Primary met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Responder Rate, Safety
evidence.text: >=50% drop-seizure responder: 0.7 mg/kg 25.3% (22/87) vs placebo 10.3% (9/87). Decreased appetite 25%, fatigue 16%. No cardiac valvulopathy/pulmonary hypertension on echo surveillance through 14 weeks.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 92% completion. D4 LOW. D5 LOW - SAP locked; mandated echo surveillance.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 2 Years; max age 35 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or non-pregnant, non-lactating female, age 2 to 35 years, inclusive as of the day of the Screening Visit.~* Clinical diagnosis of Lennox-Gastaut syndrome, where seizures that result in drops are not completely controlled by current antiepileptic treatments.~* Onset of seizures at 11 years of age or younger.~* Abnormal cognitive development.~* Must be receiving at least 1 concomitant AED and up to 4 concomitant anti-epileptic treatments.~Key Exclusion Criteria:~* E...

title: RapidMeta Neurology | Fenfluramine for Dravet and Lennox-Gastaut Syndrome v1.1
